title: Transaction Control on Informix
text: Transaction Control on Informix If there is no transaction logging, the commit and rollback instructions are ignored. When there is transaction logging, both of these instructions are supported by Informix. Never use commit and rollback in an SQL string with the sql ProcScript statement when you are using the single connection mechanism. If you end a transaction on a logon path, that transaction ends on all paths accessed by the connector. Multiple concurrent transactions are supported with the multiple connection mechanism, in which each connection has its own transaction. Global transactions, in which a single transaction spans databases over multiple connections, are not supported by Informix. Two-Phase Commit This is supported transparently by Informix. Related Links Transaction Control A transaction is a complete, logical unit of work. It usually involves adding, deleting or updating one or more logically related records in one or more databases. With Uniface, you can explicitly define a transaction with the commit ProcScript instruction. If an error occurs, a transaction can be rolled back with the rollback ProcScript instruction.
